post_rendered:
Osmium_ wrote:
EulerNTM wrote:
Commonly used Mafia Terms (from MU)
Created by EulerNTM
#
#-Shot - When a role can only be used a certain amount of times during a game, with # representing that number.

3P - Third Party - Players that are not aligned with the town or mafia.

A
AI - Alignment Indicative - an action or post by a player that IS indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia

Angleshooting - Angleshooting or to "angleshoot" is violating the spirit of the rules without actually breaking the rules. Angleshooting is not technically cheating, but is considered equally unacceptable. Explained in depth on the forum rules post.

Anti-spew - Usually used to refer to when a mafia-aligned player is very close to being killed and goes into “anti-spew”, where they are careful about their wording so that town are not able to determine who is/isn’t their partner(s) after they flip mafia.

AtE - Appeal to Emotion - When a player makes a post in order to evoke sympathy so that they can avoid being hanged.

B
Bandwagon - When player(s) hop onto a vote wagon.

BM - Short for "Bastard Mod", who can lie to players about game mechanics (including in their role PM), uses a relatively screwed setup where players might change roles, alliances, and sometimes with messed up, new roles. Generally not good for a newbie game.

BP - Bulletproof - When a player is immune to attack(s) during the night phase or during ITAs, either because they have a bulletproof modifier or a bulletproof vest.

BPV - Bulletproof Vest - A vest which is bulletproof, usually only takes one shot.

Breadcrumbing - When a player leaves clues as to what their role is/what their night actions are, in case they die without the information being revealed.

Bussing - when a mafia-aligned player votes or pushes a fellow mafia-aligned player in order to appear as if they are town-aligned whenever the mafia-aligned player they are voting has their role revealed.

C
CC - Counterclaim - when a player claims the same role as another player.

Cemetery - a place where dead players can talk about the game freely. Also known as the graveyard, DVC, or Dead Villager Chat.

CFD - Chinese Fire Drill - When a group of players suddenly switch their vote somewhere else at last minute, usually another player who wasn’t seen as an option for the hang at that point in the phase.

Claim Fishing - When a player tries to get another player to reveal their role, usually done in a subtle fashion.

Crossvoting - when two people are voting on each other. Usually in reference to MYLO or LYLO states where if Person A is voted by Person B, then either the game is over if they're both town (as mafia-aligned players can vote over Player B with majority) or there's confirmed a mafia-aligned player between the two of them.

Counterwagon - the person with the second highest amount of votes other than the person who was hanged, or in larger or more intricate games a person with enough votes on them to be considered in contention for the hang.

D
D1/D2… etc. - Day phase

DADV - Dead Air, Dead Villager - When communication in the thread has gone down, so players infer that it’s because the hang is a villager.

Derpclear - When someone seemingly shows a lack of knowledge about an open/semi-open setup, or any other information that a mafia-aligned player would be expected to know, and is then cleared for this reason by another player.

Distancing - When two mafia-aligned players try to make it seem like they are not aligned with each other.

DVC - Dead Villager Chat, otherwise known as the graveyard or cemetery. Despite the name, dead wolves are generally allowed to participate.

E
EOD - End of Day

F
F3/F5/F# - A point in the game at which # of players are alive. Generally, but not always, either used to describe a LYLO or MYLO situation or one where there is otherwise significantly less players alive than there were at game start.

Fakeclaiming - When a player claims a role that they do not have.

Flavor - The game’s theme, sometimes what roles are based off of, and creates a story usually based on what happens in the game.

Fluffposting/Fluffing - When a player posts nothing of content.

FPS - Fancy Play Syndrome - When a player uses an elaborate strategy over a more simple one.

Frozen - When a player has stopped posting, often after being called out as mafia in the thread.
Alternative: Thread is frozen and no one is posting.

G
Gambit - Any play which is made in order to evoke a certain reaction, which can be made from both alignments.

Gamethrowing - When a player actively makes an action to intentionally throw the game for their alignment; examples of this can include mafia hardclaiming mafia without a strategic reason and/or revealing who the mafia team is in the thread.

GOAT - Greatest of All Time

H
Hammer - A vote that’s placed on a player which causes the wagon to reach majority in order to hang the player that the wagon is on, ending the day phase after the vote is placed.

Hard Claim - to claim a role with there being no uncertain terms - see Soft Claim, is relevant.

Hedging - When a player does not commit to a stance and stays on the fence regarding a topic, usually the alignment of a player.

Hydra - An account shared by two or more players that share a role and have their own private chat.

I
ISO - Isolating a specific player’s posts in order to look at all of them in one place.

ITA - In Thread Attack - A mechanic used in most mashes where every player has a X% hit rate shot they may use in the thread during designated times to attempt to kill someone.

ITT - In The Thread

J
JOAT - Jack Of All Trades - A role where there are multiple (usually 1-use actions) that the player can use. Can be either mafia-aligned or town-aligned.

K
KP - Kill Power - A role of either town or mafia alignment that has the power to kill another player at night.

L
LHF - Low Hanging Fruit - A player relatively easy to get hang as either alignment.

Lolcatting - When a mafia-aligned player is confirmed mafia, often mechanically through night actions, and starts posting random stuff.

LYLO - Hang or Lose - A game state where the town must hang a non-town-aligned player or they lose. This is because the town has majority by only one player.

M
Meta - Meta overall refers to the shared history and knowledge of each other's playing styles that comes from having played with each other across several games. A meta read is when someone relies on their knowledge of another player's past behavior in a certain alignment in order to form an informed read.

Modkill - When a player is killed by the host or moderator for rule breaking during a game. This can include breaking the rules on OGI, angleshooting, or behavioral issues.

Multi - A secondary account of someone else. Any multi's found on AoPS are permabanned.

Multi-ISO - Isolating the posts of multiple people so that only the posts of the selected people are outputted.

MYLO - Mishang and Lose - A gamestate where the town must hang a mafia-aligned player or vote for no hang in order not to lose. Town has majority by two players in this gamestate.

N
N1/N2… etc. - Night phase

NAI - Not Alignment Indicative - an action or post by a player which is not indicative of whether they are aligned with the town or mafia.

NK - Night Kill - The player(s) who were killed that night, either by mafia-aligned players or town-aligned players with killing power.

NL - No Hang - When no player is hang at the end of the day phase. A common strategy used during MYLO to force the mafia to kill another town member, narrowing down the pool of suspects.

O
Openwolving - When a player talks in a way that obviously indicates mafia behaviour.

OMGUS - Oh My Gosh You Suck - When a player votes someone because the other person voted them.

P
Peek - When a town investigative uses their night action on another player, and gets information on their alignment at the end of the night phase.

Pocketing - when a player tries to gain the townread of another player, usually made by mafia attempting to pocket town

POE - Process of Elimination - A strategy which attempts to identify players who aren’t cleared, and hang amongst those uncleared players in order to find mafia-aligned players. A list of who one wishes to hang are also often referred to as PoE.

PR - Power Role - A role that is not vanilla, and has special abilities in the setup. Can refer to both mafia-aligned and town-aligned players.

Q
N/A

R
Roleclaim - To claim your role in the thread, and is used as a tactic to avoid a hang or reveal that you are a PR.

RVS - Random Voting Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get the game started by randomly voting until solid evidence builds up. Often lasts until the middle of the first day phase, sometimes ends sooner.

RQS - Random Questioning Stage - Occurs at the start of the first day phase, and is used to get general information about players, like gender or past games. Often ends after all players have answered the questions, sometimes ends sooner.

RWSO - Rand Wolf, Sub Out - A belief that a player who rands mafia, or a specific player in general, is more likely to sub out after being randed in a mafia-aligned slot, when compared to being randed as a town aligned slot. Note that subbing out in a way that is impacted by alignment is against site rules, and so is speculation on why someone subbed out; people generally use this in a non-serious manner, and you shouldn’t be using it in a game.

S
Scum - Another word for mafia-aligned players

Scumbaiting - When a town-aligned player purposely acts like a PR in order to attract the night kill.

Scumhunting - When a player tries to deduce which players are mafia-aligned.

SOD - Start of Day

Soft Claim - to imply that you are a role without directly stating it in the thread. Also can be considered breadcrumbing - see Hard Claim, semi-relevant.

Spewing - Used to refer to when a flipped mafia-aligned player has given away information about who is or isn’t a member of the mafia (or when they're doing it while still alive).

T
Thunderdome - Two players attempting to get each other hanged with at least one attempting to force the game to hang between the two until one of them flips mafia, often a term used in LYLO where two players have already crossvoted; can also be shortened to "tdome".

Tinfoil - A theory or thought that relies on something highly unlikely being the case, often entertained or brought up out of paranoia.

TMI - Too Much Information - This is used as reasoning for someone being a wolf. It revolves around the idea that the post(s) the person is making are too well-informed, so they must be mafia since the mafia already know who the rest of the mafia are.

Tunnel - When a player is focusing on one other player, accusing only them of being scum and generally not trying to solve the rest of the game.

TWTBAW - Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf - This concept is where one person appears to be so suspicious that they seem too suspicious to be mafia-aligned, hence being Too Wolfy To Be A Wolf. This is often attributed to the fact that they would be more self-conscious about being so wolfy, and would be attempting to appear more like a town member if they were actually mafia-aligned.

U
N/A

V
VCA - Vote Count Analysis - Vote count analysis is a common strategy using previous votecounts to try to find town or scum, typically looking at player composition of wagons on certain players and what those players flipped, or if they have changed their vote to save certain players.

VT - Vanilla Townie - A town role with no special abilities.

Vanilla - A role with no special abilities (both Mafia Goon and Vanilla Townie fall under this category)

Villager - Another word for town-aligned player.

W
Wagon - Refers to the number of votes a player has that makes them a candidate for the hang.

WIFOM - Wine In Front Of Me - Term originating from this scene in The Princess Bride movie. Used to denote when someone is trying to guess or reason what choice a person made, when that person knew in advance that his/her choices would be subject to scrutiny.

WIL - Wants It Less - Refers to when players are showing a lack of desire to win. Often jokingly or mockingly used to denote players who played poorly.

WIM - Wants It More - Refers to the attribute of extreme passion/desire to win that some players possess.

WOAT - Worst of All Time

Wolf - Another word for mafia-aligned players.

X
N/A

Y
N/A

Z
N/A

This is often attributed to the fact that they would be more self-conscious about being so wolfy, and would be attempting to appear more like a town member if they were actually mafia-aligned.[/hide]
[hide=U]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=V]

[b]VCA[/b] - [b]Vote Count Analysis[/b] - Vote count analysis is a common strategy using previous votecounts to try to find town or scum, typically looking at player composition of wagons on certain players and what those players flipped, or if they have changed their vote to save certain players.

[b]VT[/b] - [b]Vanilla Townie[/b] - A town role with no special abilities.

[b]Vanilla[/b] - A role with no special abilities (both Mafia Goon and Vanilla Townie fall under this category)

[b]Villager[/b] - Another word for town-aligned player.[/hide]
[hide=W]

[b]Wagon[/b] - Refers to the number of votes a player has that makes them a candidate for the hang.

[b]WIFOM[/b] - [b]Wine In Front Of Me[/b] - Term originating from [url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=U_eZmEiyTo0]this scene in The Princess Bride movie[/url]. Used to denote when someone is trying to guess or reason what choice a person made, when that person knew in advance that his/her choices would be subject to scrutiny.

[b]WIL[/b] - [b]Wants It Less[/b] - Refers to when players are showing a lack of desire to win. Often jokingly or mockingly used to denote players who played poorly.

[b]WIM[/b] - [b]Wants It More[/b] - Refers to the attribute of extreme passion/desire to win that some players possess.

[b]WOAT[/b] - Worst of All Time

[b]Wolf[/b] - Another word for mafia-aligned players.[/hide]
[hide=X]

N/A[/hide]

N/A[/hide]
[hide=Z]

N/A[/hide]
[/hide]

post_rendered:
Thoughts on flagbearer
Don't read this if you think flagbearer choice is WIFOM, because it really isn't as pure WIFOM as people think and there's probably some stuff we can attempt to try in order to potentially solve flagbearer.

In general, I agree with Osmium's theory that scum's flagbearer choice is much, much more likely to land on a experienced, non-LHF player then landing on a LHF for WIFOM purposes. If you want a simple explanation for why, it's really just Occam's razor, because in the end, when scumteam knows that people are definitely still going to be hunting for scumminess in general LHF's are much more at risk of being hanged. Letting your LHF flagbearer be a "DADV" wagon is equally as insane and there really isn't any way I see this happening to scumteam.

Out of my PoE, here are the most likely flagbearer carriers:

cw (just due to his experience)
snow (because snow isn't LHF as town, and is probably the best choice of a flagbearer not named cw anyways)
candy (did well in 225, could potentially make it through D1 on her own)
mathical (can be relatively level 0 scumminess, not a great choice for flagbearer)
HF (was relatively scummy in 220, probably not a good choice for flagbearer)
andy (LHF to the rest of the newbies, probably not a good choice for flagbearer)

Since we already talked about how insane it would be if HF was flagbearer due to the DADV on his wagon, we can cross him off the flagbearer list.
Also technically Scipio would be the number 1 flagbearer choice but his read is still pending.

I guess I could go ahead and try to pre-flip interactions and I might try it if I feel good but I'll probably be working on my reads in general today. Might land a vote on the top of the pile (cw/snow/candy) depending on my reads.

Thoughts on flagbearer
Don't read this if you think flagbearer choice is WIFOM, because it really isn't as pure WIFOM as people think and there's probably some stuff we can attempt to try in order to potentially solve flagbearer.

In general, I agree with Osmium's theory that scum's flagbearer choice is much, much more likely to land on a experienced, non-LHF player then landing on a LHF for WIFOM purposes. If you want a simple explanation for why, it's really just Occam's razor, because in the end, when scumteam knows that people are definitely still going to be hunting for scumminess in general LHF's are much more at risk of being hanged. Letting your LHF flagbearer be a "DADV" wagon is equally as insane and there really isn't any way I see this happening to scumteam.

cw (just due to his experience)
snow (because snow isn't LHF as town, and is probably the best choice of a flagbearer not named cw anyways)
candy (did well in 225, could potentially make it through D1 on her own)

HF (was relatively scummy in 220, probably not a good choice for flagbearer)
andy (LHF to the rest of the newbies, probably not a good choice for flagbearer)

experienced players

candy

Well, since you haven't been around the forum recently, lemme fill you in.

Osmium has basically become a standard NK kill. He's grown to the point where scumteam kills him regardless because of his general experience and how he can solve scumteam if he is town, regardless if he's inactive or not. Honestly I would be more surprised to see Osmium staying alive and someone else dying.

I can also say that if scumteam has Scipio or doesn't have Scipio, killing Osmium is like their best bet, for either a fearkill or attempt to scatter paranoia among the experienced players. If they did the latter, well, they are doing quite a good job at it.

Since scumteam didn't kill their flagbearer, I assumed flagbearer wasn't in like mok/HF/candy/andy just because of how LHF they are and how risky it could be to keep any of them alive, plus the fact that I don't think scumteam ever choose a flagbearer in that group anyways.

That's basically where I'm at with the Osmium NK. Not much you can really juice out of the kill but it narrows down flagbearer choices.

mok is a villager. some of the things he does like the points system and some things he asks are just useless, but he posts he has not shied away from posting after being

euler, I wouldn't be as wow surprised to see him flip wolf, but honestly, bet the game against it.he's thinking about the game, moving the game forward with his engagement, has similar stances or stances that make a lot of sense to me on most things, only the violet read is really wonky

scipio im always slightly paranoid of because of osmium kill, but beyond that, him and ming are both really solid v's. they're a tier lower because I would still seriously think about them for a long time in lylo, this is more of a "what I think the poe for the course of the rest of the game looks like" post.

socoobo, cw, have operated as like "voice of reason" slots, which isnt necessarily ai, but ive kinda liked socoobo for it and disliked cw for it as ive reread, idk why. socoobo definitely the far more villagery. I havent fact checked the basis of his corgi read, but I really really really like it.. it looks a lot like some relatively early big pushes ive tended to make; the reasons are nice. ONLY paranoia wrt socoobo is the one wolf in three newbies stance granted literally no effort to find which of the newbies was actually the wolf once he thought he had that obvious lead. cw has just dropped off the face of the earth recently which I think in a day like this day phase especially compared to last is a really bad look. literally his last 72 hours are like "huh why did I vote corgi?" *24 hours* "I still dont know why I voted corgi" "oh!" the end but getting no attention at all still

corgi seems seriously struggling recently, he had a sort of "alright, whatever, kill me" moment but then just TOTALLY rolled over, I remember literally nothing he's said since then

aroodman, totally slots into my worldview etc but I want to give him self resolving opportunity, I think seeing whether he dies and if before me can in late game say a lot about who was right about the state of the game with very little room for wifom, etc. I'll add that im extra paranoid because with players of higher omg he subbed in reputation, if I come into a wolf slot, literally my only concern tends to be "how will I explain why im alive in lylo", and my answer is always just "I'll end up wolf reading my bros and have rly bad reads so 'haha no wonder'". if ive at all been right about the game so far then aroodman would have plenty of excuses for still being here in lylo. also I dont think I talked enough about how blatantly wolfy Andy was before he subbed out; it's partly why ive had negative preconceptions about this slot. Andy is, as a villager, literally capable of trying to be helpful, having thoughts about the game. his spamming mok w.. a) this is clearly something he didnt believe, you definitely cant convince me otherwise, b) he didnt think the spamming was useful. i do tend to feel like im looking at this slot with confbias so hopefully I can reevaluate when I wake up tomorrow with a clear mind.

Thoughts on sheep:

Read the entire HF situation again and yeah the DADV and general absence is still towny. Also the fact that HF just gave up in his last post is probably slightly town indicative for someone like HF (in 220, where he was scum, he didn't want to give up until he tried to get into the 223 playerlist, which really doesn't count). Sheep's reads coming into the game are understandable, like his mok/violet scum theater thing, while it isn't the right read, is still good, so I'm fine with that. The fact that he also doesn't want to PL mok because of alignment reasons is something I feel as well.
Sheep's 180 of me is...interesting because it kinda goes against what he originally thought of me. I mean, it started kinda gradually and now it's just "f it, he's wolf" kinda thing. It's not really towny or scummy because I can see how it can come from either scum!sheep or town!sheep.
I think like, base read is still sheep v. It's just really hard to see the DADV coming from scum!HF of all people.

aroodman:

I agreed that Andy was extremely off-meta and that was scummy in itself, and I can't exactly ignore that. Some of aroodman's reads were somewhat similar to mine, although I disagree with both the cw and snow reads and I'm not sure where he gets that from. There was also the issue about how aroodman only focused on like one section of my 500-ish post iso when 221 exists. His treatment of mok is somewhat towny.
I mean, the main issue I have with aroodman here is that while he has a couple towny moments, andy was scummy and some of his reads don't make much sense to me and yeah, I'm kinda having him at null right now.

I haven't done pre-flip because it's not a healthy way of doing things (I tried and failed in 221).

So yeah, I think PoE should be {aroodman, cw, snow}. Every post in a game of mafia can be the birthplace for endless outcomes. A momentary argument, a day’s elimination, the entire game’s result... can all be swung completely by just one post.
